commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an ACP `agent:turn:end` internal hook event, emits it from the ACP manager after runtime turn termination, documents the lifecycle-only contract, and adds focused manager/hook plus gateway fixture tests.

PR surface: Source +102, Tests +259, Docs +3. Total +364 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this PR adds a new hook capability. The PR does include a high-confidence live terminal proof path for the new behavior using a source-built Gateway and real ACPX/Codex turn.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; the next action is maintainer review of the new hook contract and its separation from the sibling save-outcome hook.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent turn end hook
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a clean `agent:turn:end` internal hook event for ACP runtime turn completion. This split intentionally keeps the event as a runtime lifecycle boundary only; it does not claim transcript/session-write durability.

## Changes
- Registers and validates `agent:turn:end` hook events with `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` context.
- Emits the event from the ACP manager after terminal runtime turn handling and awaited ACP event-delivery callbacks.
- Does not emit `agent:turn:end` for pre-runtime session initialization failures such as max-concurrency rejection.
- Documents the lifecycle-only contract and explicitly states that this event does not prove durable session writes.
- Adds focused ACP manager and internal hook tests for success, failure, ordering, and pre-runtime init-failure gating.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: proves the clean split branch emits `agent:turn:end` from an actual ACP runtime turn, while keeping save/write-durability concerns out of this PR and avoiding turn-end emission for pre-runtime init failures.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at `/Users/mantis/Projects/openclaw-pr-turn-end-clean`, branch `feat/agent-turn-end-hook-clean`, rebased onto current `origin/main`, head `52d28708c0`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the source-built Gateway from this branch with a temporary config/state directory, enabled the embedded `acpx` runtime plugin, loaded a temporary internal hook through `hooks.internal.load.extraDirs`, initialized a real ACPX/Codex persistent session using a temporary copied `CODEX_HOME`, ran `AcpSessionManager.runTurn()` with the prompt `Reply with exactly TURN-END-HOOK-PROOF-OK`, then attempted a second runtime session while `acp.maxConcurrentSessions` was `1` to force a pre-runtime `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` path.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```text
$ git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD
52d28708c0
$ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_CODEX_MODEL=gpt-5.5 \
  OPENCLAW_TURN_END_SOURCE_CODEX_HOME=/Users/mantis/.codex \
  OPENCLAW_TURN_END_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD) \
  node --import tsx .artifacts/turn-end-proof-run.mjs
{
  "ok": true,
  "head": "52d28708c0",
  "initializedBackend": "acpx",
  "successSessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-proof-f0e595eb-8a63-42f0-80aa-b6e373699f0c",
  "blockedSessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-blocked-4cdae395-c82e-4e0f-b466-ef6a1e0f1486",
  "successHookContext": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-proof-f0e595eb-8a63-42f0-80aa-b6e373699f0c",
    "success": true,
    "durationMs": 4200
  },
  "blockedError": {
    "name": "AcpRuntimeError",
    "code": "ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED",
    "message": "ACP max concurrent sessions reached (1/1)."
  },
  "hookEventCount": 1,
  "blockedHookEventCount": 0,
  "proofLog": "/Users/mantis/.openclaw/state/tmp/openclaw-turn-end-proof-wuPoN6/turn-end-proof.jsonl"
}
```
- Additional focused validation:
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 6.99s
$ pnpm check:test-types
<exit 0>
$ pnpm exec oxlint src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
<exit 0>
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
<exit 0>
```
- Observed result after fix: the temporary hook captured exactly one real `agent:turn:end` event from a live ACPX/Codex turn with `success: true`; the pre-runtime max-concurrency failure returned `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` and captured zero `agent:turn:end` events for that blocked session.
- What was not tested: production gateway deployment. This was intentionally run against a temporary local Gateway/config/state using source code from this branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses local Codex auth copied into a temporary Codex home and a one-off local proof harness that is not committed; hook handlers are internal and only observable when an operator installs/enables a hook.

## Testing
- Real source-built ACPX/Codex turn proof described above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check`

## Related
Related #37833
Split from #71399
Sibling save-write outcome work: #89154

commands.author: mantisai-bot

target.title: fix(media): suppress local whisper progress transcripts
target.body:
## What

CLI audio transcription now treats deterministic transcript-file outputs as authoritative instead of falling back to progress/status stdout.

This covers:

- `whisper-cli` with `-otxt` / `--output-txt` and `-of` / `--output-file`
- `whisper` with `--output_format txt` and `--output_dir`
- `parakeet-mlx` with explicit `--output-format txt` and `--output-dir`
- the local Whisper Node wrapper output paths via `--output-dir`, `--output`, and `-o`

Empty or missing inferred transcript files now produce no transcript instead of exposing status/progress output as user text. Unexpected transcript-file read errors still surface. Other Node wrappers, parakeet default/non-txt output, and other non-authoritative CLI paths keep the existing stdout fallback behavior.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87384.

## Why

Some local speech-to-text wrappers print progress banners to stdout while writing the actual transcript to a file. When that transcript file is empty or missing, OpenClaw could previously treat the progress banner as the user transcript. That is wrong for live audio messages because wrapper status text can leak into the inbound conversation body.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Whisper-style CLI wrapper status text could be injected as an audio transcript when the expected transcript file was empty or missing.
- Real environment tested: refreshed PR head `3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858`, disposable OpenClaw staging state, stored Telegram DM voice media (`audio/ogg`) from `@kesslerClawBot` staging proof, and a controlled local-whisper-compatible wrapper that printed Whisper progress to stdout, wrote an empty inferred transcript file under `{{OutputDir}}`, and exited successfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: merged current `origin/main` into the PR branch, then ran a current-head direct media runtime proof through `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts` / `runCliEntry` with `node <proof-wrapper> <stored-telegram-audio.ogg> --output-dir <tmp>`. The same wrapper/media path was previously exercised through the disposable Telegram gateway with `@kesslerClawBot`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): public proof gist with current-head direct runtime output plus the redacted live Telegram gateway excerpt: https://gist.github.com/kesslerio/97110dff154bef75ddb80f35c5cdc8e8

  ```txt
  PR #87393 current-head runCliEntry proof
  head=3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858
  media=<staging-state>/media/inbound/ec282e95-e8cd-4472-b8ce-d15d73cac548.ogg
  wrapper=<staging-state>/proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js

  result null
  PASS: runCliEntry returned null; local-whisper wrapper progress stdout was not exposed as transcript text.
  ```

  Prior live Telegram gateway excerpt for the same wrapper behavior:

  ```txt
  audio-preflight: transcribing attachment 0 for mention check
  Media understanding via CLI: node <staging-state>/proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js <staging-state>/media/inbound/ec282e95...ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-...
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output
  telegram inbound: chatId=<redacted> from=telegram:<redacted> len=92 preview="[Telegram ...] <media:audio>"
  ```

- Observed result after fix: current-head `runCliEntry` returned `null` for the empty authoritative transcript file; in the live Telegram proof, OpenClaw logged wrapper progress output but kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the current-head proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling on the changed source path; the live Telegram proof was captured before the current-main merge, but the refreshed PR diff still only changes the same five media/doc/test files against current `origin/main`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/nodes/audio.md docs/nodes/media-understanding.md src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests
- current-head direct runtime proof passed and is linked above

Validation limitation:

- `timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` failed after the current-main merge on declaration errors in unrelated current-main files for `proper-lockfile`, `cross-spawn`, and `hosted-git-info`. No media-understanding files were reported in that failure.

## CI Status

CI is running on current head `3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858`.

The branch now includes current `origin/main` (`7d5d62511f`) and contains `scripts/github/dependency-guard.mjs`, so the previous Dependency Guard failure from checking out `53ad531df9` without that script should be cleared by this head.

## Maintainer Edits

Maintainer edits are enabled on this fork PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted implementation and review. Human-run proof was captured through the disposable staging gateway and supplemented with a current-head direct runtime proof. Local review included scoped simplify/code-review passes and focused media-understanding validation.

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Codex](https://img.shields.io/badge/Codex-000000)

target.title: fix(media): suppress local whisper progress transcripts
target.body:
## What

CLI audio transcription now treats deterministic transcript-file outputs as authoritative instead of falling back to progress/status stdout.

This covers:

- `whisper-cli` with `-otxt` / `--output-txt` and `-of` / `--output-file`
- `whisper` with `--output_format txt` and `--output_dir`
- `parakeet-mlx` with explicit `--output-format txt` and `--output-dir`
- the local Whisper Node wrapper output paths via `--output-dir`, `--output`, and `-o`

Empty or missing inferred transcript files now produce no transcript instead of exposing status/progress output as user text. Unexpected transcript-file read errors still surface. Other Node wrappers, parakeet default/non-txt output, and other non-authoritative CLI paths keep the existing stdout fallback behavior.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87384.

## Why

Some local speech-to-text wrappers print progress banners to stdout while writing the actual transcript to a file. When that transcript file is empty or missing, OpenClaw could previously treat the progress banner as the user transcript. That is wrong for live audio messages because wrapper status text can leak into the inbound conversation body.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Whisper-style CLI wrapper status text could be injected as an audio transcript when the expected transcript file was empty or missing.
- Real environment tested: refreshed PR head `3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858`, disposable OpenClaw staging state, stored Telegram DM voice media (`audio/ogg`) from `@kesslerClawBot` staging proof, and a controlled local-whisper-compatible wrapper that printed Whisper progress to stdout, wrote an empty inferred transcript file under `{{OutputDir}}`, and exited successfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: merged current `origin/main` into the PR branch, then ran a current-head direct media runtime proof through `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts` / `runCliEntry` with `node <proof-wrapper> <stored-telegram-audio.ogg> --output-dir <tmp>`. The same wrapper/media path was previously exercised through the disposable Telegram gateway with `@kesslerClawBot`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): public proof gist with current-head direct runtime output plus the redacted live Telegram gateway excerpt: https://gist.github.com/kesslerio/97110dff154bef75ddb80f35c5cdc8e8

  ```txt
  PR #87393 current-head runCliEntry proof
  head=3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858
  media=<staging-state>/media/inbound/ec282e95-e8cd-4472-b8ce-d15d73cac548.ogg
  wrapper=<staging-state>/proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js

  result null
  PASS: runCliEntry returned null; local-whisper wrapper progress stdout was not exposed as transcript text.
  ```

  Prior live Telegram gateway excerpt for the same wrapper behavior:

  ```txt
  audio-preflight: transcribing attachment 0 for mention check
  Media understanding via CLI: node <staging-state>/proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js <staging-state>/media/inbound/ec282e95...ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-...
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output
  telegram inbound: chatId=<redacted> from=telegram:<redacted> len=92 preview="[Telegram ...] <media:audio>"
  ```

- Observed result after fix: current-head `runCliEntry` returned `null` for the empty authoritative transcript file; in the live Telegram proof, OpenClaw logged wrapper progress output but kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the current-head proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling on the changed source path; the live Telegram proof was captured before the current-main merge, but the refreshed PR diff still only changes the same five media/doc/test files against current `origin/main`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/nodes/audio.md docs/nodes/media-understanding.md src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests
- current-head direct runtime proof passed and is linked above

Validation limitation:

- `timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` failed after the current-main merge on declaration errors in unrelated current-main files for `proper-lockfile`, `cross-spawn`, and `hosted-git-info`. No media-understanding files were reported in that failure.

## CI Status

CI is running on current head `3f383768675217b456189d972867a1d0bdde7858`.

The branch now includes current `origin/main` (`7d5d62511f`) and contains `scripts/github/dependency-guard.mjs`, so the previous Dependency Guard failure from checking out `53ad531df9` without that script should be cleared by this head.

## Maintainer Edits

Maintainer edits are enabled on this fork PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted implementation and review. Human-run proof was captured through the disposable staging gateway and supplemented with a current-head direct runtime proof. Local review included scoped simplify/code-review passes and focused media-understanding validation.

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Codex](https://img.shields.io/badge/Codex-000000)
target.author: kesslerio

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
